meta_title: For Founders: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Shipping
content.seo_description: Learn how to use the Antigravity project template to overcome unfinished projects. A technical case study on building faster with Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop the cycle of unfinished projects by leveraging the Antigravity project template. By standardizing your architecture and automating boilerplate with Claude Code, you shift from 'coding' to 'product shipping.' This approach forces modularity, ensures technical debt is minimized, and keeps your MVP focused on core revenue-generating features.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'perfect' codebases that never saw a single user. You've been there: the initial spark, the feverish sprint of week one, and then the slow, agonizing death of feature creep. You are drowning in context switching and structural indecision, convinced that one more refactor will finally make it 'ready.'

It’s not your technical skill holding you back; it’s the lack of an execution framework. The Antigravity project template transforms your development process from a chaotic exploration into a high-velocity production line. By pairing this rigid, battle-tested structure with the autonomous capabilities of Claude Code, you eliminate the friction that causes founders to abandon projects mid-stream.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the root architecture. This ensures your project follows a production-grade directory structure that separates concerns immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project using the Antigravity standard template, focusing on a Next.js/Supabase stack with built-in authentication and stripe integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project scaffold using Antigravity template for Next.js/Supabase'

steps.title: Define the Core Business Logic
steps.description: Map out your primary revenue-driving module. By focusing on the 'Happy Path' first, we ensure the project remains viable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the core domain logic for a subscription-based document processor, following the Antigravity service-layer pattern.

steps.title: Automate API Route Generation
steps.description: Use the template's built-in hooks to generate type-safe API routes, reducing manual boilerplate and potential runtime errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create CRUD API routes for the document processor module, ensuring full type safety and error handling as per Antigravity standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate type-safe API routes for document processor based on existing domain logic'

steps.title: Implement Database Schemas
steps.description: Sync your database schema with your logic layer to ensure data integrity from the start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the Supabase schema definitions that align with the document processor service layer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate and apply SQL migrations for document processor schemas'

steps.title: End-to-End Testing & Deployment
steps.description: Run the automated verification suite to confirm the MVP is shippable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the CI/CD pipeline for the project and run the standard Antigravity integration tests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure CI/CD and execute full test suite'

pro_tips: Never build custom infrastructure for authentication or payments; use the Antigravity built-in integrations to save 40+ hours.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; force it to update the README every time a new module is created.
Treat your MVP as a 'disposable' prototype; if a feature takes more than 4 hours to implement, simplify the requirements.
Always keep your business logic in a separate package from your UI components to allow for rapid pivoting.
Run a 'kill-switch' audit weekly: delete any code or feature that doesn't directly contribute to your core value proposition.
content.next_steps: After completing this scaffold, deploy to Vercel and set up your Stripe dashboard. Focus entirely on user acquisition for the next 7 days without touching the codebase.
content.conversion_block: You have the framework, the tools, and the strategy. Stop tinkering and start shipping value that users will pay for. The difference between a hobbyist and a founder is the ability to finish. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
